ORDER FOR STAY OF EXECUTION STAYED UNTIL APR-14-1998, $1,175.00 DEPOSITED AS RENT

DEPT. 04; GEORGE T. CHOPPELAS, JUDGE; M. BRADY, CSR. SPECIFICATION OF DECISION - PURSUANT TO SECTION 657 OF THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SAID MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL WAS GRANTED FOR THE FOLLOWING REASONS: 1. ERROR IN LAW, OCCURING AT THE TRIAL AND EXCEPTED TO BY PARTY MAKING APPLICATION. PREVIOUS OFFER OF PROOF CONCERNING PAYMENT OF RENT UNCLEAR AS TO IF ACCEPTED BY PLAINTIFF SUBSEQUENT TO EXPIRATION OF 30 DAY NOTICE. ACCEPTANCE OF RENT AFTER TERMINATION OF TENANCY CREATED A NEW TENANCY. 2. INSUFFICIENCY OF THE EVIDENCE TO JUSTIFY THE DECISION, AND OTHER DECISION IS AGAINST LAW. FAILURE TO PRESENT TESTIMONY THAT 30 DAY NOTICE WAS PROPERLY SERVED ON DEFENDANTS.
1ST REQUEST SENT TO REPORTER FOR ESTIMATE
NOTICE OF APPEAL FILED (NOT A CROSS APPEAL) BY DEFENDANTS AVRUTIN AND KUTCHUK FROM ORDER ENTERED ON MARCH 30, 1998.
APPEAL FILED BY DEFENDS. ON 4-29-98 FROM ORDER 3-30-98. DEPOSITED BY DEFENDANT AVRUTIN, ALEXKANDR AVRUTIN, FAINA KUTCHUK, GIDEON

REMITTITUR FILED WHILE THIS CASE WAS ON APPEAL, RESPONDENTS, ALEKSANDR AND FAINA AVRUTIN, FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY. PURSUANT TO BANKRUPT- CY CODE (11 U.S.C. 362), ALL ACTIONS ARE STAYED AS TO THESE RESPONDENTS. THEREFORE, THIS ORDER IS ONLY APPLICABLE TO THE REMAINING RESPONDENT, GIDEON KUTCHUK.

REMITTITUR FILED ORDER RECALLING REMITTITUR AND AMENDING PRIOR ORDER FROM SUPERIOR COURT; THE COURT WAS UNAWARE AT THE TIME OF THE ORDER THAT DEFENDANT GIDEON KUTCHUK, HAD ALSO PETITIONED FOR BANKRUPTCY. DUE TO THIS MISTAKE OF FACT, THE REMITTITUR IN THIS CASE IS RECALLED AND THE PRIOR ORDER IS AMENDED TO STAY ENFORCEMENT AGAINST GIDEON KUTCHUK PURSUANT TO BANKRUPTCY CODE (11 U.S.C. 362)

REMITTITUR FILED THE TRIAL COURT GRANTED THE NEW TRIAL ON THE GROUNDS THAT AN ERROR OF LAW HAD OCCURRED AT TRIAL AND THAT THERE WAS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO JUSTIFY THE DECISION. THIS COURT FINDS THAT GRANTING THE NEW TRIAL WAS NOT AN ABUSE OF DIS- CRETION. THE COURT HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT AND REVIEWED THE EVIDENCE, LAW AND ARGUMENT AFFIRMS THE ORDER GRANTING A NEW TRIAL.
